skill.name: turborepo-caching
skill.description: Configure Turborepo caching, task pipelines, CI filters, and remote cache workflows for JavaScript monorepos.
skill.summary: Set up Turborepo cache strategy, CI filtering, and remote cache workflows for faster monorepo builds.
skill.author: wshobson

security_audit.summary: Static analysis reported many command, network, filesystem, environment, and obfuscation patterns, but most are false positives from markdown templates and command examples. The confirmed risks are documentation-level issues in the self-hosted cache example: unvalidated filesystem paths, missing authentication, and disabled remote cache signatures. No prompt injection attempt or confirmed malicious intent was found.

medium_findings.title: Unvalidated Cache File Paths in Example Server
medium_findings.description: The self-hosted cache example builds file paths from request parameters and query values before reading and writing cache files. A copied implementation should validate teamId and hash values and constrain paths to the cache directory.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The TypeScript example joins CACHE_DIR with request-controlled teamId and hash values before file reads and writes. This is strong evidence of path handling risk in the published template, although the skill itself does not execute the code.

medium_findings.title: Remote Cache Example Lacks Server-Side Authentication
medium_findings.description: The cache server example exposes artifact GET, PUT, and HEAD routes without checking a token or team authorization. Users who copy it could publish an unauthenticated cache endpoint.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The client command includes a token placeholder, but the server example has no corresponding token verification. The risk is clear for copied deployments, but it remains documentation-level code.

medium_findings.title: Remote Cache Signature Disabled in Template
medium_findings.description: The self-hosted cache configuration disables remote cache signatures. This can weaken artifact integrity protections if users apply it without compensating controls.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The template explicitly sets signature to false for remote caching. This is a real configuration choice, but the final risk depends on deployment controls outside the skill.

low_findings.title: Sensitive Environment Files Included in Cache Inputs
low_findings.description: The Turborepo template lists .env and .env.local as global dependencies. This may cause secret-bearing files to influence cache keys, so users should avoid storing secrets in committed cache inputs.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The file references are explicit, and the skill also warns against including secrets in environment values. The risk is limited because there is no evidence of secret exfiltration.

low_findings.title: Static Analyzer Code Execution Alerts Are Documentation Examples
low_findings.description: The backtick execution, weak cryptography, and critical obfuscation alerts are not confirmed as malicious behavior. The detected locations are markdown fences, shell examples, schema URLs, and cache terminology in a documentation-only skill.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Review found no executable skill scripts, prompt injection, hidden download behavior, or credential exfiltration. The suspicious tokens appear inside explanatory markdown examples.

dangerous_patterns.title: Unvalidated Cache File Paths in Example Server
dangerous_patterns.description: The self-hosted cache example builds file paths from request parameters and query values before reading and writing cache files. A copied implementation should validate teamId and hash values and constrain paths to the cache directory.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The TypeScript example joins CACHE_DIR with request-controlled teamId and hash values before file reads and writes. This is strong evidence of path handling risk in the published template, although the skill itself does not execute the code.

dangerous_patterns.title: Remote Cache Example Lacks Server-Side Authentication
dangerous_patterns.description: The cache server example exposes artifact GET, PUT, and HEAD routes without checking a token or team authorization. Users who copy it could publish an unauthenticated cache endpoint.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The client command includes a token placeholder, but the server example has no corresponding token verification. The risk is clear for copied deployments, but it remains documentation-level code.

dangerous_patterns.title: Remote Cache Signature Disabled in Template
dangerous_patterns.description: The self-hosted cache configuration disables remote cache signatures. This can weaken artifact integrity protections if users apply it without compensating controls.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The template explicitly sets signature to false for remote caching. This is a real configuration choice, but the final risk depends on deployment controls outside the skill.

content.value_statement: Monorepo builds often waste time by rebuilding unchanged packages. This skill guides Turborepo cache setup, CI filtering, and remote cache patterns.
actual_capabilities: Draft Turborepo pipeline configurations for build, test, lint, typecheck, and dev tasks.
Show remote caching setup patterns for Vercel and self-hosted cache servers.
Provide CI workflow examples that use Turborepo filters for changed packages.
Explain cache inputs, outputs, dependencies, persistent tasks, and cache misses.
Suggest package-level overrides and advanced pipeline rules for monorepos.
limitations: It provides templates and guidance, not a running Turborepo configuration tool.
Self-hosted cache examples require security hardening before production use.
It does not inspect a repository unless the assistant is asked to read project files.
Version-specific Turborepo behavior may require checking current official documentation.

use_cases.title: Set Up a New Monorepo Cache
use_cases.description: Create a Turborepo task pipeline with explicit outputs, inputs, and dependency order for predictable caching.

use_cases.title: Speed Up CI Builds
use_cases.description: Apply remote cache variables and changed-package filters so CI runs fewer tasks after each pull request.

use_cases.title: Diagnose Cache Misses
use_cases.description: Use dry runs, verbosity, graph output, and summaries to understand why tasks rebuild unexpectedly.

prompt_templates.title: Create a Basic Pipeline
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner setup for a new Turborepo workspace.
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a Turborepo cache configuration for apps and packages with build, test, lint, typecheck, dev, and clean tasks.

prompt_templates.title: Add CI Remote Cache
prompt_templates.scenario: Intermediate setup for pull request builds.
prompt_templates.prompt: Design a GitHub Actions workflow that uses Turborepo remote caching, secrets, team variables, and changed-package filters.

prompt_templates.title: Tune Package Overrides
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced configuration for mixed application and package builds.
prompt_templates.prompt: Review my monorepo tasks and propose package-specific Turborepo overrides for Next.js apps, shared packages, and database generation.

prompt_templates.title: Audit Cache Behavior
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced debugging for slow or inconsistent builds.
prompt_templates.prompt: Analyze cache misses in my Turborepo project and recommend safer inputs, outputs, env settings, filters, and diagnostic commands.

output_examples.input: A team wants faster pull request checks in a Turborepo monorepo.
output_examples.output: A CI plan that installs dependencies once, restores remote cache, builds only changed packages, and runs affected tests.

output_examples.input: A Next.js app keeps missing cache after environment changes.
output_examples.output: A short diagnosis that separates public build variables from runtime secrets and narrows cache inputs to stable files.

output_examples.input: A platform team wants to run a self-hosted remote cache.
output_examples.output: A production checklist covering authentication, path validation, artifact integrity, storage isolation, logging, and retention.

best_practices: Define outputs and inputs explicitly for tasks that produce build artifacts or coverage reports.
Use remote caching in CI with least-privilege tokens and protected secret storage.
Run diagnostic commands before changing cache rules so cache misses have clear evidence.
anti_patterns: Do not cache long-running development servers or deployment tasks with side effects.
Do not place secret values in cache inputs, public environment variables, or shared artifacts.
Do not deploy the self-hosted cache example without authentication, path validation, and integrity checks.

faq.question: What does this skill help configure?
faq.answer: It helps configure Turborepo pipelines, cache inputs, cache outputs, CI filters, and remote cache workflows.

faq.question: Can it improve CI build speed?
faq.answer: Yes. It suggests remote cache setup and filters that limit work to changed packages and their dependencies.

faq.question: Does it run Turborepo commands automatically?
faq.answer: No. It provides command examples and configuration guidance for an assistant or developer to apply.

faq.question: Is the self-hosted cache template production ready?
faq.answer: No. It needs authentication, authorization, path validation, artifact integrity checks, and operational controls.

faq.question: Does it support Claude, Codex, and Claude Code?
faq.answer: Yes. The report lists Claude, Codex, and Claude Code as supported tools for this skill.

faq.question: When should cache be disabled?
faq.answer: Disable cache for dev servers, deployment tasks, database push tasks, and other commands with side effects.
